title: Email and Phone Number Validation System
summary: A Python function that validates email addresses and phone numbers based on specific rules and formats.

user_stories.title: Validate an email address
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to check if an email address is valid so that I can ensure it is correctly formatted and belongs to a valid domain.
acceptance_criteria: The function returns True if the email is valid
The function returns False if the email is invalid
The function checks for proper format (e.g., username@domain.tld)
The function checks for valid domain (e.g., no invalid characters in domain name)

user_stories.title: Validate a phone number
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to check if a phone number is valid so that I can ensure it follows a standard format.
acceptance_criteria: The function returns True if the phone number is valid
The function returns False if the phone number is invalid
The function supports international and local phone number formats
The function checks for correct length and allowed characters

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The validation function should execute quickly even for large volumes of data
The validation logic must be accurate and follow standard validation rules
The code should be well-documented and easy to maintain

constraints: The function must be implemented in Python and should not rely on external libraries for core validation logic.
assumptions: The input strings will be in standard text format. The function will handle common edge cases such as leading/trailing spaces.
out_of_scope: The system does not include checking if an email or phone number actually exists or is registered with a service.
